## [00:00 - 03:01]

G'day, welcome back. Mike here from agenciesavvy.com and in this module we're going to talk about campaigns. Now this is a fundamental building block to any AdWords account. We saw in the last module that you have an AdWords account that is made up of one or more campaigns and then the campaign is where you collect all of your Ad Groups and each Ad Group contains one or more ads and one or more keywords. So why would you have more than one campaign? Let's start there. Where you might have different types of campaign, you might have one for the search network, one for your shopping ads and one for your banner ads. That might be one reason that you need more than one. Now we're not going to talk about shopping and display in this particular course. Loads of stuff on that at agenciesavvy.com if you really want to learn more about shopping and display. We've got tons of stuff on that. Another reason that you might have more than one campaign is you might have one for each location. That might be one for each country. So you might have a campaign for the states, a campaign for Australia, a campaign for New Zealand, a campaign for the UK and so on. You may even have a campaign for each state that you're in. So if you're selling into half a dozen states in the US then you might have one campaign for California and one campaign for Texas and one campaign for New Mexico, whatever it might be. If you sell different products that might be another good reason. So you could have lots of different ad groups stuffed into the one campaign but it's probably going to be cleaner and easier to manage if you had one campaign for the doors that you sell and one for the windows and one for the awnings and one for the shutters. It's probably just going to make life a little easier because there's so many different things here but it would give you more control then and really this is what the game is all about here, giving you the maximum level of control so that you can get the best possible result. One setting, we're going to talk about settings in just a second but one setting that we set, that's a really bad sentence, four campaigns is your budget. So let's go back to the doors and windows and awnings example. If you found that your ads for windows were absolutely crushing it and you were getting leads left right and centre at a really really good price but doors were super expensive. If we lumped all of those ad groups together in the same campaign we wouldn't really get much control over where the money got spent. If we separate those two things out into two campaigns now I can

adjustment of minus 100 percent that's how you turn mobiles off in ad words it's kind of different to facebook i know but if you wanted to turn traffic off on mobiles and if you can choose you can choose to turn mobile traffic off on tablets too in which case you need a bid adjustment of minus 100 percent for mobile and minus 100 percent for tablet in order to turn mobile and tablet off by default you're able to be running across all three devices and bidding the same across all three devices so it's up to you how you set those and budgets we've covered just set whatever budget is sensible for you so that you're not going to melt a credit card you're not going to lose your shirt we don't want that to happen so i'll see you in the next module and we're talking about conversion tracking super super important before you start running ads we need a way of knowing what is working and what's not so i'll see you for that in the next module
